post_rendered:
Mafia Game 215: Insanity Mafia

credits go to all the usual people for the rule template
  • Flavor
    Closed setups are scrubby so have an open one instead
  • Rules
    • Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may use your role PM to ask for rule clarifications.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any communication from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless you are specifically given permission to do so.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread.
    • Do not gamethrow. This is a modkill level infraction.
    • Do not angleshoot.
    • Do not flake.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • Hide tags are allowed as long as they conform to the above rule.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted, even for things like typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • Do not try to ruin the game. Doing so might result in a ban from joining games.
    • Do not abuse loopholes.

  • Voting and Hanging
    • This game is based on deadline hang. This means that the player with the greatest number of votes at the deadline will be hanged.
    • If there is a tie at the deadline, the hang will be randomly chosen from the players with the greatest number of votes.
    • Starting Day 2, if a player reaches a majority of votes, then that player will be hanged.
    • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: sudzoof" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: sudzOOf", "Vote: sudzoof", "Vote sudzoof", “vote: sudzoof, "Vote:sudzoof", "Vote: sudfooz", "Vote: sudzooof", and "Vote: sudzoof" are not.
    • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly and do not use nicknames.
    • Votes in quotes or hide or tip tags will not be counted.
    • Votes are locked in Lylo.
    • Properly made votes will be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game and someone accidentally hammered.
    • Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until the mods say the game is officially over. If you want to purposely fake vote, give the mods a heads up in your role PM so it is not accidentally counted.
    • You may vote for a no hang to occur during day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • If there is an even number of players, half of the players voting for no hang is considered a majority, but only for no hang.
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, but if you wish to, unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • The moment the deadline passes (or majority is reached, if it is enabled), no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post, and the game will proceed into twilight.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once the end-of-day scene is posted, the person hanged is officially dead and their role will be revealed. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • Put effort into the game. The moderators will prod you first and then replace you (48 hours after a prod) if you don't play the game. Generally, you will be prodded if you do not post for 48 hours.
    • If the moderators get tired of repeatedly prodding you, you'll be replaced.
    • Each day will be 6 days (144 hours). The moderators may extend the deadline for the end of the day at their discretion. Note that this will be unlikely and will usually only happen in circumstances of extreme inactivity or replacements too close to end of day.
    • The night deadline will be 1 day after night begins.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or their partner(s) (if they have them), the player will lose their chance to perform an action during that night.
    • Any night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end some time after the night deadline when the mods make the post announcing the end of night. You may not post until the moderators say you can, even if the night deadline has passed.
    • If a player is going to be in V/LA (vacation/limited access; i.e., less available to play the game for some time), they must post in bold in-thread or PM the mods. The moderators will not prod the player during the V/LA unless the V/LA is too long.
    • Mafia do not have daychat.
    • Do not excessively spam for the sake of spamming. If you are an outed wolf, for example, don't spam to clog up the thread (a bit of fun is fine, but excessive spam is not). In particular, don't spam just because you're mad or annoyed at getting hanged.

  • Etiquette
    •  Do not use inappropriate language.
    •  Do not make any personal attacks.
    •  Follow all of the rules.
    •  Be mature.
    •  For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to three warnings, SE’s up to two, and IC’s one. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill-level infraction, they will be modkilled or force-replaced at the mods' discretion. Please do not do anything bad enough to deserve a modkill.

  • Moderators
    • sudzoof is the moderator of Mafia Game 215 and cw357 is the co-moderator.
    • Don't post in color. If you do have to post in color, try to avoid green, blue, red, and purple. (using these colors is fine as long as your post doesn't resemble a mod post)
    • The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error to the votecount is not pointed out before the next votecount, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time.
    • The moderators have the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Setup
    This setup is open. It consists of:
    • 1 Town Cop
    • 6 Vanilla Townies
    • 2 Mafia Goons

    Town Cop has a n0 check.

    This game may be BM.
  • Alive(3)
    1. awesomeming327
    2. kevinmathz
    3. VKU427 Thornclaw9000 Fvr_Vain
  • Dead(6)
    BZ1, hung Day 1, was a Vanilla Townie.
    b137740 bigmath, killed Night 1, was a Vanilla Townie.
    correcthorsebatterystaple, hung Day 2, was a Mafia Goon.
    aroodman, killed Night 2, was a Vanilla Townie.
    snoozingnewt, hung Day 3, was a Vanilla Townie.
    Osmium_, killed Night 3, was a Town Cop.
  • Role PMs
    Quote:
    Welcome, [Player Name], you are a Town Cop.

    Each night phase, you may target one player in the game to investigate them. You will receive a result of "Town" or "Mafia."

    You win if all threats to the town are eliminated and at least one town-aligned player is alive.
    Quote:
    Welcome, [Player Name], you are a Vanilla Townie.

    You have no abilities.

    You win if all threats to the town are eliminated and at least one town-aligned player is alive.
    Quote:
    Welcome, [Player Name], you are a Mafia Goon.

    Your partner is [player], and you may discuss with them in this QuickTopic during the night while you are alive. Together, you may submit a factional kill each night to eliminate a player.

    You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or if nothing can prevent this
Do not post until this line is crossed out.

Night 3 ends on October 1, 10:30 PM EDT

post_rendered:
1. He
2. Your choice
3. Idrk
4. The intention implicit in the question is to be told the number of degrees in a full rotation. However, the question asks how many degrees there are in a "circle", which is defined as the set of points equidistant from one chosen point, as opposed to an angle equal to one rotation. A circle can be represented as a path or just a set, but not an angle. Though the implied answer is , at no point along the path while travelling continuously at a constant speed and not returning to any point twice is there a angle; instead, as the path is differentiable, there are only angles. This question made the fatal error of attempting to apply the concept of angles in a context where there was no clearly-defined point where the angle should be, and the person who asked this question should avoid making similar mistakes in the future by contemplating similar questions in solitude for extended periods of time, the same way I always contemplate everything, due to my lack of friends.
post_canonical:
1. He
2. Your choice
3. Idrk
4. The intention implicit in the question is to be told the number of degrees in a full rotation. However, the question asks how many degrees there are in a "circle", which is defined as the set of points equidistant from one chosen point, as opposed to an angle equal to one rotation. A circle can be represented as a path or just a set, but not an angle. Though the implied answer is $360^\circ$, at no point along the path while travelling continuously at a constant speed and not returning to any point twice is there a $360^\circ$ angle; instead, as the path is differentiable, there are only $180^\circ$ angles. This question made the fatal error of attempting to apply the concept of angles in a context where there was no clearly-defined point where the angle should be, and the person who asked this question should avoid making similar mistakes in the future by contemplating similar questions in solitude for extended periods of time, the same way I always contemplate everything, due to my lack of friends.

awesomeming327. wrote:
???????????????????????
Vote: aroodman for being confusing

I can see how that might have been confusing, here's an explanation:

The intention implicit in the question is to be told the number of degrees in a full rotation. However, the question asks how many degrees there are in a "circle", which is defined as the set of points equidistant from one chosen point, as opposed to an angle equal to one rotation. A circle can be represented as a path or just a set, but not an angle. Though the implied answer is , at no point along the path while travelling continuously at a constant speed and not returning to any point twice is there a angle; instead, as the path is differentiable, there are only angles. This question made the fatal error of attempting to apply the concept of angles in a context where there was no clearly-defined point where the angle should be, and the person who asked this question should avoid making similar mistakes in the future by contemplating similar questions in solitude for extended periods of time, the same way I always contemplate everything, due to my lack of friends.

Why is ming's username misspelt in the OP

On a slightly more serious note, yeah where are chbs and VKU. On an even more slightly more serious note, please slow down a bit on the fluff, a little fun is fine but this could end up being annoying for chbs and VKU to read when they get here.

I can see how that might have been confusing, here's an explanation:

The intention implicit in the question is to be told the number of degrees in a full rotation. However, the question asks how many degrees there are in a "circle", which is defined as the set of points equidistant from one chosen point, as opposed to an angle equal to one rotation. A circle can be represented as a path or just a set, but not an angle. Though the implied answer is $360^\circ$, at no point along the path while travelling continuously at a constant speed and not returning to any point twice is there a $360^\circ$ angle; instead, as the path is differentiable, there are only $180^\circ$ angles. This question made the fatal error of attempting to apply the concept of angles in a context where there was no clearly-defined point where the angle should be, and the person who asked this question should avoid making similar mistakes in the future by contemplating similar questions in solitude for extended periods of time, the same way I always contemplate everything, due to my lack of friends.

I don't even know why I don't like it. In theory it's good but ehhh, I feel like info is bound to leak. In one mafia game on unofficial WSM, everyone just bandwagon-ed n0 check on one person so obviously real cop would cop would have a harder time.

And so to combat this:

IF YOU ARE NOT COP, THEN USE RNG TO DETERMINE YOUR N0

There, no WIFOM and no info loss.

@snoozing, cop doesn't pick who they checked n0, which you would have figured out if you just read sudzoof's post that the n0 check is always town.
